channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: HCoC Youth Group Fellowship 2026–2027: Fully Funded Global Programme on Missile Non-Proliferation and Security Studies
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The HCoC Youth Group Fellowship 2026–2027 is a prestigious international programme designed for young professionals, graduate students, and early-career researchers interested in global security, arms control, and non-proliferation. Organized by the Fondation pour la Recherche Stratégique (FRS) with support from the European Union, this fellowship provides a unique platform to engage with leading experts, participate […]

The post HCoC Youth Group Fellowship 2026–2027: Fully Funded Global Programme on Missile Non-Proliferation and Security Studies appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The HCoC Youth Group Fellowship 2026–2027 is a prestigious international programme designed for young professionals, graduate students, and early-career researchers interested in global security, arms control, and non-proliferation. Organized by the Fondation pour la Recherche Stratégique (FRS) with support from the European Union, this fellowship provides a unique platform to engage with leading experts, participate in high-level discussions, and contribute to policy-relevant research on ballistic missile proliferation.

This second edition builds on the success of its inaugural cohort and responds to an increasingly complex global security environment characterized by rapid technological advancements, shifting geopolitical dynamics, and the growing spread of missile technologies.

About the HCoC Youth Group Programme

The HCoC Youth Group is part of a broader initiative supporting the Hague Code of Conduct against Ballistic Missile Proliferation (HCoC), an international effort aimed at promoting transparency, confidence-building, and restraint in missile development.

The programme is structured as an 18-month fellowship that combines:

  • Virtual learning and discussions
  • In-person engagement with experts
  • Independent research and publication

Participants will join a global cohort of 15 fellows, creating a diverse and interdisciplinary learning environment.

Programme Structure and Activities

The HCoC Youth Group Fellowship offers a comprehensive and interactive learning experience.

1. Expert Webinars

Fellows will participate in four thematic webinars scheduled across the programme timeline:

  • November 2026
  • February 2027
  • April 2027
  • June 2027

Each session includes:

  • Expert presentations by researchers, diplomats, and practitioners
  • Moderated discussions
  • Pre-submitted questions from fellows

These sessions provide deep insights into missile non-proliferation and related global security issues.

2. Virtual Cafés

Optional informal sessions will be held throughout the programme to:

  • Foster peer interaction and collaboration
  • Discuss current global developments
  • Share research progress and ideas

These sessions promote a collaborative and supportive learning environment.

3. In-Person Workshop (Lithuania)

A key highlight of the programme is a three-day residential workshop scheduled for September 2027 in Plokštinė, Lithuania (subject to confirmation).

Key features include:

  • Expert briefings and discussions
  • Research exchange sessions
  • Simulation exercises (e.g., negotiation scenarios or war games)
  • Guided visit to a former Cold War missile base

All travel and accommodation costs for this workshop are fully covered by the programme.

4. Individual Research and Publication

Each fellow is required to produce an individual research paper as part of the programme.

Key aspects include:

  • Selection of a research topic aligned with programme themes
  • Development of an analytical paper
  • Contribution to a collective publication edited by FRS

This publication serves as a key output of the programme and enhances participants’ academic and professional profiles.

Thematic Pillars

The programme is structured around two core thematic pillars:

Pillar 1: Technical Dimension

Focus areas include:

  • Emerging trends in missile proliferation
  • Dual-use technologies and space systems
  • Intersections between civilian and military technologies
  • Implications for arms control frameworks

Pillar 2: Diplomatic Dimension

Focus areas include:

  • Evaluation of the Hague Code of Conduct
  • International cooperation and policy responses
  • Challenges in a multipolar geopolitical landscape
  • Recommendations for strengthening global non-proliferation efforts

Applicants are required to indicate their preferred thematic focus during the application process.

Eligibility Criteria

The programme is open to a diverse group of candidates with relevant academic or professional backgrounds.

Who Should Apply

  • Young professionals
  • Graduate students (Master’s level or above)
  • Early-career researchers

Required Qualifications

  • Demonstrated interest in one or more of the following areas:
    • Ballistic missile technology
    • Arms control and strategic studies
    • WMD non-proliferation
    • Space policy and governance
    • International law and diplomacy
    • Regional security studies
  • Full working proficiency in English
  • Availability to participate in all programme activities
  • Age requirement: Applicants must be under 40 years old

Selection Criteria

The selection committee will evaluate candidates based on:

  • Quality and clarity of application
  • Relevance of academic or professional background
  • Geographic diversity
  • Gender balance
  • Interdisciplinary representation

Time Commitment

The programme is designed to be flexible while maintaining high engagement.

  • Estimated commitment: 4–6 hours per month
  • Additional time required for research and workshop preparation

Why You Should Apply

This fellowship provides a rare opportunity to engage with global security issues at a high level while developing research, analytical, and policy skills. It is particularly valuable for individuals aiming to build careers in international relations, defense policy, diplomacy, or strategic studies.

Application Process

Applicants must submit:

Required Materials

  • Completed online application form
  • Curriculum Vitae (maximum 2 pages)

Application Questions Include

  • Motivation for joining the programme
  • Key challenges in missile proliferation
  • Contribution to the group
  • Thematic interest
  • A key question for experts

All submissions must be in English.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the HCoC Youth Group Fellowship 2026–2027 is:

July 31, 2026

Applicants will be notified of the outcome within four weeks after the deadline. Find the Call for Applications document here.

Applications must be submitted through the online form available here.

Programme Benefits

Participation in the programme offers numerous benefits, including:

  • Free participation (no programme fees)
  • Fully funded travel and accommodation for the in-person workshop
  • Access to leading experts and policymakers
  • Opportunity to publish research work
  • Global networking and collaboration
  • Practical experience in international security and diplomacy

Terms and Conditions

  • Fellows participate in a personal capacity
  • All research outputs will be published under participants’ names
  • Active participation throughout the programme is mandatory
  • Non-participation may lead to removal from the programme

Contact Information

For inquiries, applicants can contact:

m.garcia-carrasco@frstrategie.org

Learn more about the HCoC Youth Group Fellowship HERE.

Discover more global Fellowship and Research opportunities on OFY.

The post HCoC Youth Group Fellowship 2026–2027: Fully Funded Global Programme on Missile Non-Proliferation and Security Studies appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Advance Your Research Career: Postdoctoral Research Associate Role at Texas A&M University
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Are you a PhD graduate in economics or a related field looking to work on impactful global research? The Postdoctoral Research Associate position at Texas A&M University offers an exciting opportunity to contribute to cutting-edge research on conflict, forced displacement, and economic development. Based in College Station, Texas, this role allows you to collaborate with […]

The post Advance Your Research Career: Postdoctoral Research Associate Role at Texas A&M University appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a PhD graduate in economics or a related field looking to work on impactful global research? The Postdoctoral Research Associate position at Texas A&M University offers an exciting opportunity to contribute to cutting-edge research on conflict, forced displacement, and economic development. Based in College Station, Texas, this role allows you to collaborate with leading researchers, work with rich datasets, and engage in policy-relevant research. Applications are open now, with a deadline of 15 April 2026.

About the Opportunity

The Program on Conflict and Development (ConDev) within the Department of Agricultural Economics at Texas A&M University is seeking a Postdoctoral Research Associate to join its research team. ConDev conducts advanced research on the economic causes and consequences of conflict, forced displacement, and recovery, combining theoretical frameworks with empirical analysis.

This position offers the opportunity to work closely with the program director, Dr. Harun Onder, and collaborate with affiliated researchers on ongoing and new research initiatives. The program’s work spans a wide range of topics, including conflict spillovers, labor market responses to instability, the effects of forced displacement on host communities, and the relationship between climate shocks and economic systems.

Researchers in this program use diverse and complex data sources, including administrative microdata, household and firm surveys, and geospatial datasets. The selected candidate will contribute to data analysis, research design, and the development of academic outputs, as well as participate in seminars, workshops, and broader intellectual activities within the department.

The initial appointment is for one year, with the possibility of renewal based on performance and funding. The anticipated start date is 1 June 2026, making this an excellent opportunity for recent PhD graduates seeking to transition into a strong academic or policy-oriented research career.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Understanding the economic impacts of conflict and displacement is critical for designing effective policies and interventions. Research in this field helps governments, international organizations, and development agencies respond to crises, support recovery, and build resilient economies.

The ConDev program at Texas A&M University plays a key role in advancing this knowledge by combining rigorous academic research with real-world policy engagement. By joining this program, researchers contribute to work that has direct implications for global development and humanitarian efforts.

This opportunity is especially valuable for early-career researchers who want to engage in policy-relevant, data-driven research while building a strong academic track record.

What You’ll Gain

This role provides a range of professional and academic benefits:

  • Advanced Research Experience: Work on high-impact research addressing global challenges such as conflict and displacement.
  • Collaboration Opportunities: Engage with leading researchers and contribute to interdisciplinary projects.
  • Data Expertise: Gain experience working with complex datasets, including microdata and geospatial information.
  • Career Development: Strengthen your academic profile through research publications and project contributions.
  • Intellectual Environment: Participate in seminars, workshops, and academic discussions within a dynamic research community.

This position is ideal for candidates looking to build a strong foundation in applied economic research and contribute to meaningful policy discussions.

Eligibility

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Hold a PhD in economics or a closely related field by the start date
  • Have strong training in applied econometrics
  • Demonstrate experience working with microdata
  • Be proficient in causal inference methods, such as panel data analysis, difference-in-differences, or instrumental variables

Preferred qualifications include:

  • Experience with geospatial or spatial data analysis
  • Familiarity with administrative or firm-level datasets
  • Interest in research related to developing countries or conflict-affected settings
  • Experience working with large or complex datasets

Application Process and Deadline

To apply for the Postdoctoral Research Associate position:

  1. Prepare Application Materials: Include a cover letter, CV, writing sample, and contact information for three references.
  2. Submit Application Online: Apply through the official Texas A&M careers portal.
  3. Deadline: Ensure your application is submitted by 15 April 2026.

To learn more and apply, visit the official Texas A&M Postdoctoral Research Associate post.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Advance Your Research Career: Postdoctoral Research Associate Role at Texas A&M University appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: CoFSA Remote Internship 2026 on Food Systems & Global Summit Coordination
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Are you passionate about sustainability, food systems, and global change? The UNDP Conscious Food Systems Alliance (CoFSA) Internship 2026 offers a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience while contributing to global efforts to transform food systems. This fully remote internship allows you to work with an international team, support a major global summit, and build […]

The post Apply Now: CoFSA Remote Internship 2026 on Food Systems & Global Summit Coordination appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about sustainability, food systems, and global change? The UNDP Conscious Food Systems Alliance (CoFSA) Internship 2026 offers a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience while contributing to global efforts to transform food systems. This fully remote internship allows you to work with an international team, support a major global summit, and build valuable skills in coordination, research, and community engagement. Applications are open now and close on 10 April 2026.

About the Opportunity

The Conscious Food Systems Alliance (CoFSA), convened by the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), is a global movement bringing together practitioners, organizations, and changemakers working to transform food systems. The initiative focuses not only on technical solutions but also on developing the inner capacities—such as collaboration, awareness, and leadership—needed to drive systemic change.

As part of its growing global work, CoFSA is organizing the first Global Conscious Food Systems Summit, scheduled to take place in Bhutan in 2026. This landmark event will bring together stakeholders from across sectors to advance dialogue and action on sustainable, inclusive, and resilient food systems.

To support this effort, UNDP is recruiting a Convening & Community Intern who will contribute to both the operational and community-building aspects of the initiative. The role is based within UNDP’s Bureau for Policy and Programme Support (BPPS) under the Nature, Climate, and Energy portfolio, which works globally to advance sustainable development and the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

This internship provides exposure to global policy processes, multi-stakeholder collaboration, and international development work, making it highly valuable for those interested in sustainability and global impact careers.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Food systems are central to global development, impacting health, economies, climate, and livelihoods. However, current systems face major challenges, including environmental degradation, food insecurity, and inequality. Transforming these systems requires not only technical solutions but also new ways of thinking and collaboration.

The CoFSA initiative addresses this by focusing on systems change and human-centered approaches, making it a unique and forward-thinking program within the UN ecosystem. By joining this internship, you contribute to a global effort aimed at creating resilient, inclusive, and sustainable food systems.

This role is particularly valuable for young professionals who want to gain experience within the United Nations system, understand global policy frameworks, and actively contribute to achieving the Sustainable Development Goals.

What You’ll Gain

This internship offers a range of meaningful benefits:

  • Global Experience: Work with UNDP and contribute to international initiatives on food systems transformation.
  • Hands-On Learning: Gain practical experience in coordination, research, communications, and community engagement.
  • Summit Exposure: Support the planning and execution of a major global summit in Bhutan.
  • Professional Development: Build skills in project management, stakeholder engagement, and knowledge management.
  • Global Network: Engage with diverse partners, practitioners, and changemakers from around the world.

This opportunity is ideal for individuals seeking to build a career in international development, sustainability, or policy.

Eligibility

Applicants must meet one of the following educational requirements:

  • Be enrolled in the final year of a bachelor’s degree, or
  • Be enrolled in a graduate program, or
  • Have graduated within the past year

Relevant fields include communications, environmental studies, agriculture, sustainable development, economics, international development, and social sciences.

Additional requirements include:

  • Experience in research, data analysis, or communications
  • Interest or experience in sustainability or food systems
  • Strong organizational and communication skills
  • Fluency in English
  • Ability to work independently in a remote, global environment

Application Process and Deadline

To apply for the CoFSA Internship:

  1. Prepare Your Application: Ensure your CV and academic details are up to date.
  2. Apply Online: Submit your application through the official UNDP careers portal.
  3. Review Process: Applications will be reviewed, and shortlisted candidates may be contacted for further steps.

Location: Remote (Home-based)
Duration: 6 months
Application Deadline: 10 April 2026

To learn more and apply, visit the official CoFSA Internship job posting.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Apply Now: CoFSA Remote Internship 2026 on Food Systems & Global Summit Coordination appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded COP31 Climate Fellowship 2026 in Türkiye: A Life-Changing Global Opportunity for Young Leaders from the Global South
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The global climate movement is entering a decisive era, and one of the most powerful opportunities for young changemakers has officially opened: the Fully Funded COP31 Fellowship Program 2026 by Life of Pachamama. This prestigious international opportunity is designed to empower young leaders from the Global South to actively participate in global climate negotiations and […]

The post Fully Funded COP31 Climate Fellowship 2026 in Türkiye: A Life-Changing Global Opportunity for Young Leaders from the Global South appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The global climate movement is entering a decisive era, and one of the most powerful opportunities for young changemakers has officially opened: the Fully Funded COP31 Fellowship Program 2026 by Life of Pachamama. This prestigious international opportunity is designed to empower young leaders from the Global South to actively participate in global climate negotiations and shape the future of environmental governance.

What Is the COP31 Fellowship Program?

The COP31 Fellowship is a highly competitive, fully funded international program created to prepare and support young climate leaders from the Global South to participate in the 31st Conference of the Parties (COP31) under the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change.

This initiative is not just about attending a conference—it is a multi-phase leadership and advocacy program that equips participants with the tools, knowledge, and networks required to influence global climate policies.

The fellowship focuses on:

  • Climate justice and equity
  • International policy and negotiation processes
  • Strategic communication and advocacy
  • Community-driven climate action

Participants are trained to become influential voices representing marginalized and underrepresented communities on the global stage.

Why COP31 Matters in 2026

The COP31 will take place in Antalya, Türkiye from November 9–20, 2026 . This global summit is one of the most important decision-making platforms on climate action.

At COP31:

  • Countries will evaluate progress toward the Paris Agreement goals
  • New climate finance strategies will be implemented
  • Global emissions reduction plans will be assessed
  • Critical decisions affecting the future of the planet will be made

This means fellows will not just observe—they will engage directly in high-level negotiations and discussions shaping global climate policy.

Program Structure: A Transformational 3-Phase Journey

1. Virtual Training Phase (May – June 2026)

Participants undergo an intensive online training program covering:

  • Climate governance and diplomacy
  • Policy analysis
  • Climate justice frameworks
  • Communication strategies

The training uses a “learning by doing” approach and promotes peer collaboration and real-world application.

2. International Advocacy Phase (COP31 in Türkiye)

Selected fellows will travel to Antalya to:

  • Participate in COP31 sessions
  • Engage with global policymakers and organizations
  • Represent their communities in international discussions
  • Build networks with climate leaders worldwide

This phase provides direct exposure to multilateral negotiations and global coordination processes.

3. Local Impact Phase (Post-COP Implementation)

After returning home, fellows must:

  • Launch community-based climate initiatives
  • Share knowledge gained during the fellowship
  • Promote climate awareness and grassroots action

This ensures that the program creates lasting local impact beyond the global stage.

Fully Funded Benefits

One of the strongest aspects of this opportunity is its comprehensive financial support. Selected fellows will receive:

  • Round-trip international airfare
  • Accommodation in Türkiye
  • Daily stipend for meals and local transport
  • International health insurance
  • Access to expert mentorship and training
  • Global networking opportunities

This means no financial barriers for selected participants.

Who Can Apply? (Eligible Countries and Categories)

This fellowship specifically targets young people from the Global South, particularly from the following countries:

  • Colombia
  • Mexico
  • Peru
  • Brazil
  • Indonesia
  • Ghana
  • Gabon
  • Uganda
  • Tanzania

Eligible Categories of Applicants

Applicants are encouraged from:

  • Indigenous communities
  • Afro-descendant populations
  • Rural and peasant communities
  • Migrants and refugees
  • Youth from marginalized or conflict-affected regions
  • Women, LGBTIQ+ individuals, and persons with disabilities

The program prioritizes historically excluded and underrepresented groups.

Eligibility Criteria and Requirements

To qualify, applicants must:

Age Requirement

  • Be between 15 and 30 years old

Language Skills

  • Speak English or Spanish (basic to intermediate level recommended)

Experience

  • Demonstrate involvement in:
    • Climate action
    • Environmental advocacy
    • Social or community initiatives

Commitment

  • Be available for all phases of the program
  • Dedicate time to training and post-program activities

Additional Requirements

  • Willingness to implement a local impact project
  • Ability to engage in international dialogue

What Makes This Fellowship Unique?

Unlike many global programs, the COP31 Fellowship stands out because:

1. Focus on the Global South – It amplifies voices that are often excluded from global decision-making.

2. Real Participation in Global Negotiations – Participants don’t just attend—they actively engage in climate diplomacy.

3. Full Financial Support – Everything from travel to accommodation is covered.

4. Long-Term Impact – The fellowship ensures that knowledge gained is applied locally.

5. Strong Global Network – Participants connect with:

  • International organizations
  • Policymakers
  • Climate activists

Application Process

The application process is simple but competitive:

  • Complete an online application form (approx. 30 questions)
  • Provide personal details and experience
  • Answer questions about climate perspectives and motivation
  • Submit within the deadline

The process takes about 5 minutes to complete, but thoughtful responses are critical for selection.

Apply here

Key Deadline

30th April 2026

The COP31 Fellowship by Life of Pachamama is more than just an opportunity—it is a gateway to global influence, leadership, and impact. At a time when the world urgently needs climate solutions, this program empowers young people to:

  • Shape international climate policies
  • Represent their communities globally
  • Drive sustainable change locally

If you are committed to climate justice and want to be part of a transformative global movement, this fellowship could define your future.

For more information click here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Fully Funded COP31 Climate Fellowship 2026 in Türkiye: A Life-Changing Global Opportunity for Young Leaders from the Global South appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Lead Global Change: Apply for the MYCP Global Coordinator Role (Remote, Full-Time)
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Are you ready to lead global efforts that amplify the voices of young migrants? The Migration Youth & Children Platform (MYCP) is now recruiting a Global Coordinator to drive its next phase of growth and impact. This fully remote, full-time leadership role offers a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of youth advocacy, migration […]

The post Lead Global Change: Apply for the MYCP Global Coordinator Role (Remote, Full-Time) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you ready to lead global efforts that amplify the voices of young migrants? The Migration Youth & Children Platform (MYCP) is now recruiting a Global Coordinator to drive its next phase of growth and impact. This fully remote, full-time leadership role offers a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of youth advocacy, migration policy, and international collaboration—while shaping global conversations within key United Nations spaces. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, with priority given to those submitted by 12 April 2026.

About the Opportunity

The Migration Youth & Children Platform (MYCP) is a youth-led global initiative that creates space for children and young people (up to 31 years old) to actively engage in international policy processes, particularly within the United Nations system. Since its founding in 2018, MYCP has played a key role in amplifying youth voices in global migration discussions, contributing to platforms such as the Global Compact on Migration (GCM) and the Global Forum on Migration and Development (GFMD).

Following a period of internal transformation, MYCP is entering a new chapter and is seeking a Global Coordinator to lead its Secretariat. This role is central to the organization’s operations and strategic direction, ensuring that its mission to promote meaningful youth engagement remains at the forefront.

The Global Coordinator will oversee daily operations, manage teams, engage with global partners, and represent MYCP in high-level international spaces. This is a leadership role that combines strategy, operations, and advocacy, making it ideal for candidates with experience in global migration, youth engagement, and multi-stakeholder coordination.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Youth voices—especially those of migrants and displaced communities—are often underrepresented in global decision-making processes. MYCP plays a critical role in ensuring that these voices are not only heard but actively shape policies that affect them.

The Global Coordinator position is essential to sustaining and expanding this impact. By leading MYCP’s Secretariat, the selected candidate will help bridge the gap between youth perspectives and international policy-making, ensuring that global frameworks reflect real experiences and needs.

This role is particularly important at a time when migration remains a key global issue. Strengthening youth participation in these discussions contributes to more inclusive, equitable, and effective policies, benefiting communities worldwide.

What You’ll Gain

This role offers significant professional and personal development opportunities:

  • Global Leadership Experience: Lead an international youth platform engaging with UN processes and global stakeholders.
  • Career Growth: Strengthen your expertise in migration policy, advocacy, and organizational leadership.
  • Global Network: Collaborate with UN agencies, partners, donors, and youth leaders worldwide.
  • Strategic Impact: Shape initiatives that influence global migration discussions and youth participation.
  • Flexible Work Environment: Work remotely while engaging across diverse regions and time zones.

This opportunity is ideal for individuals looking to advance their leadership in global advocacy spaces and make a meaningful impact on migration policy and youth inclusion.

Eligibility

To be considered for the MYCP Global Coordinator role, applicants should meet the following requirements:

  • Strong knowledge of global migration advocacy spaces, including GCM, GFMD, and UN processes
  • Proven experience in team management, project coordination, and multi-stakeholder initiatives
  • Excellent organizational and communication skills
  • Fluency in English (additional UN languages are an advantage)
  • Ability to work independently across multiple time zones
  • Familiarity with tools such as Google Workspace, Slack, Zoom, and collaborative platforms

Desirable qualifications include lived experience of migration, experience in fundraising or donor relations, and previous involvement in youth-led or global initiatives. Prior experience with MYCP is also an advantage but not mandatory.

Application Process and Deadline

To apply for the MYCP Global Coordinator role:

  1. Prepare Your Documents: Update your CV and write a brief statement outlining your motivation and relevant experience.
  2. Complete the Application Form: Submit your application through the official MYCP form.
  3. Rolling Review: Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submission is strongly encouraged.

Read the full Job Description. To learn more and apply, visit the official MYCP Global Coordinator page.

Priority Deadline: 12 April 2026

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Lead Global Change: Apply for the MYCP Global Coordinator Role (Remote, Full-Time) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Advance Your Career with the Mayor’s Undergraduate Scholarship Program 2026 in New York City
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Are you a full-time New York City government employee looking to earn a college degree without leaving your job? The Mayor’s Scholarship Program (MSP) 2026–2027 offers undergraduate scholarships that allow eligible employees to pursue an associate or bachelor’s degree while continuing to work. With the Fall 2026 application cycle now open, this is a valuable […]

The post Advance Your Career with the Mayor’s Undergraduate Scholarship Program 2026 in New York City appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a full-time New York City government employee looking to earn a college degree without leaving your job? The Mayor’s Scholarship Program (MSP) 2026–2027 offers undergraduate scholarships that allow eligible employees to pursue an associate or bachelor’s degree while continuing to work. With the Fall 2026 application cycle now open, this is a valuable opportunity to gain higher education, improve your career prospects, and receive financial support—all while staying employed. The application deadline is April 27, 2026.

About the Opportunity

The Mayor’s Scholarship Program (MSP) is an initiative by the City of New York designed to support the professional development of its workforce. Originally focused on graduate studies, the program has expanded to include undergraduate scholarships, enabling city employees to pursue associate and bachelor’s degrees at participating colleges and universities.

This program allows employees to balance work and education, as participants continue their full-time roles while studying during their own time. Scholarship awards are provided and determined by participating institutions, making this a collaborative effort between the city and higher education providers.

The MSP is designed to make higher education more accessible to public servants by reducing financial barriers and supporting their long-term career growth. It also helps strengthen the city’s workforce by equipping employees with new skills, knowledge, and qualifications that can be applied directly to their roles.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Access to higher education can be a major barrier for working professionals, especially those balancing financial responsibilities and full-time employment. The Mayor’s Scholarship Program addresses this challenge by allowing city employees to pursue a degree without leaving their jobs.

This initiative not only benefits individuals but also strengthens public service by creating a more skilled and educated workforce. Employees can apply their new knowledge directly to their roles, improving efficiency and service delivery across city agencies.

Furthermore, by partnering with educational institutions, the program ensures that employees have access to quality academic programs while receiving financial support. It promotes lifelong learning and empowers workers to take control of their professional development.

What You’ll Gain

The Mayor’s Undergraduate Scholarship Program offers several important benefits:

  • Financial Support: Scholarships funded by participating colleges and universities to help cover tuition costs.
  • Career Advancement: Earn a recognized associate or bachelor’s degree to improve promotion opportunities.
  • Work-Study Balance: Continue working full-time while pursuing your education.
  • Skill Development: Gain new knowledge and practical skills that enhance your professional performance.
  • Long-Term Growth: Strengthen your qualifications for future roles within or beyond city government.

This opportunity is especially valuable for employees who want to advance their careers without interrupting their income or job stability.

Eligibility

To be eligible for the undergraduate scholarships, applicants must:

  1. Be a full-time New York City government employee.
  2. Have completed a high school diploma or equivalent by the start of the scholarship year (Fall 2026).
  3. Remain a full-time NYC government employee throughout the course of study.
  4. Maintain good academic standing as a student.
  5. Attend classes on their own time.

There is no minimum length of employment required, making this opportunity accessible even to relatively new employees. Additionally, recipients are not required to remain in city service after completing their degree, providing flexibility for future career decisions.

Application Process and Deadline

The application process requires careful attention to instructions and coordination with your agency:

  1. Check Participating Schools: Confirm that your chosen college or program is included in the Mayor’s Scholarship Offering Guide.
  2. Apply to the School: Submit your academic application directly to the institution if required.
  3. Complete Scholarship Application: Fill out the scholarship application for each school you are applying to.
  4. Submit to Agency Personnel Office (APO): Submit your completed application(s) to your Agency Personnel Office, not directly to the school.
  5. Agency Review: Your APO will verify your employment status and forward your application to the Mayor’s Scholarship Program office.

Applicants should confirm any internal deadlines with their agency, as some may require earlier submission.

Application Period: 30 March 2026 – 27 April 2026
Final Deadline: 27 April 2026

To learn more and apply, visit the official Mayor’s Scholarship Program website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Advance Your Career with the Mayor’s Undergraduate Scholarship Program 2026 in New York City appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNESCO Book Reviews Editor (Remote) 2026: Paid Consultancy Opportunity with €10,500 Total Compensation
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The UNESCO Institute for Lifelong Learning (UIL) is inviting applications for the role of Book Reviews Editor for its flagship academic journal, the International Review of Education – Journal of Lifelong Learning (IRE). This remote consultancy opportunity offers experienced professionals in education and research the chance to contribute to one of the world’s leading journals […]

The post UNESCO Book Reviews Editor (Remote) 2026: Paid Consultancy Opportunity with €10,500 Total Compensation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UNESCO Institute for Lifelong Learning (UIL) is inviting applications for the role of Book Reviews Editor for its flagship academic journal, the International Review of Education – Journal of Lifelong Learning (IRE). This remote consultancy opportunity offers experienced professionals in education and research the chance to contribute to one of the world’s leading journals in lifelong learning while working within the United Nations system.

This position is ideal for academics, editors, and researchers who are deeply engaged in education, lifelong learning, and scholarly publishing, and who wish to play a key role in shaping global academic discourse.

About UNESCO Institute for Lifelong Learning (UIL)

The UNESCO Institute for Lifelong Learning (UIL) is a globally recognized institution that supports UNESCO Member States in advancing lifelong learning policies and systems. Its work focuses on:

  • Learning ecosystems
  • Skills development for life and work
  • Inclusive and equitable education
  • Literacy and non-formal education

UIL hosts one of the largest specialized libraries in lifelong learning and manages the International Review of Education, one of the longest-running journals in the field since its establishment in 1955.

About the Role: Book Reviews Editor

The selected consultant will serve as Book Reviews Editor for the International Review of Education – Journal of Lifelong Learning (IRE), a peer-reviewed journal published in English by Springer.

This role is central to maintaining the journal’s academic quality, diversity, and relevance in the field of lifelong learning.

Key Responsibilities

The Book Reviews Editor will work under the supervision of the Executive Editor and contribute to both editorial and strategic aspects of the journal.

Core Responsibilities

  • Identify and select relevant books for review by:
    • Monitoring publisher catalogues
    • Consulting academic networks
    • Reviewing peer journals
  • Commission and manage book review submissions by:
    • Identifying qualified reviewers
    • Providing clear submission guidelines
    • Coordinating timelines and expectations
  • Edit and evaluate submitted reviews by:
    • Ensuring quality, clarity, and academic rigor
    • Providing feedback and revision guidance
    • Making editorial decisions on acceptance
  • Maintain reviewer database and records
  • Decide on the inclusion of reviews in each journal issue in collaboration with the editorial team

Additional Responsibilities

  • Support the peer review process for academic articles
  • Help attract high-quality research submissions
  • Participate in academic events and conferences
  • Contribute to strengthening the journal’s global reputation
  • Report to the Editorial Board and Executive Editor when required

Work Modality and Duration

  • Location: Fully remote
  • Contract Duration:
    • Initial 2-month contract starting May 2026
    • Followed by a 5-month extension (based on performance)

This flexible arrangement allows professionals worldwide to participate without relocation.

Deliverables

The consultant will be expected to:

  • Submit monthly progress reports
  • Present an annual report to the Editorial Board (November)
  • Maintain consistent editorial output and workflow

Compensation and Payment Structure

UNESCO offers a structured payment plan for this consultancy role.

Financial Details

  • Monthly Payment: €1,500
  • Total Compensation: €10,500
  • Payment Schedule: 7 installments based on deliverables and reporting

Payments are contingent upon submission of required reports and invoices.

Required Competencies

Candidates must demonstrate strong professional and interpersonal competencies aligned with UNESCO standards.

Core Competencies

  • Accountability
  • Communication
  • Planning and organization
  • Teamwork
  • Professionalism

Candidates should be capable of working independently while collaborating effectively in an international, multicultural environment. For detailed information, please consult the UNESCO Competency Framework.

Why You Should Apply

This opportunity provides a rare chance to:

  • Work with UNESCO and contribute to global education discourse
  • Gain editorial experience in a leading academic journal
  • Expand your professional network internationally
  • Influence research and knowledge in lifelong learning
  • Work remotely with flexible arrangements

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit a complete application package as a single PDF file.

Required Documents

  1. Full CV detailing:
    • Academic background
    • Publications
    • Conferences attended
    • Professional memberships
  2. Cover Letter including:
    • Motivation for applying
    • Ideas for the book reviews section
    • Explanation of suitability for the role

Incomplete applications will not be considered.

Application Process

To apply:

  • Submit your application via the UNESCO careers portal
  • Upload a single consolidated PDF (CV + Cover Letter)
  • Ensure all information is complete and accurate

Applications will be reviewed based on:

  • Relevance of experience
  • Academic and editorial expertise
  • Alignment with UNESCO values

Selection Process

The recruitment process may include:

  • Application screening
  • Competency-based assessments
  • Interviews (virtual)
  • Reference checks

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the UNESCO Book Reviews Editor role is:

April 17, 2026 (Midnight Paris Time)

Applicants are encouraged to submit early to avoid last-minute issues.

For any questions please contact: ire@unesco.org

 

APPLY NOW

Click HERE to learn more about the UNESCO Book Reviews Editor paid Consultancy Opportunity.

Discover more Job opportunities on OFY HERE.

 

The post UNESCO Book Reviews Editor (Remote) 2026: Paid Consultancy Opportunity with €10,500 Total Compensation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ESCWA Paid Fellowship Programme 2026 for Young Arab Professionals: Fully Funded UN Opportunity with $4,000 Monthly Stipend
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The ESCWA Fellowship Programme for Young Arab Professionals 2026 is a prestigious, fully funded United Nations fellowship designed to empower early- and mid-career professionals from the Arab region. Organized by the United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Western Asia (ESCWA), this programme offers a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience in policymaking, research, and […]

The post ESCWA Paid Fellowship Programme 2026 for Young Arab Professionals: Fully Funded UN Opportunity with $4,000 Monthly Stipend appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The ESCWA Fellowship Programme for Young Arab Professionals 2026 is a prestigious, fully funded United Nations fellowship designed to empower early- and mid-career professionals from the Arab region. Organized by the United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Western Asia (ESCWA), this programme offers a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience in policymaking, research, and technical cooperation while contributing to sustainable development across the Arab world.

This fellowship is not just a training programme; it is a gateway into the core of regional and international policymaking processes, providing participants with direct exposure to the work of the United Nations and its impact on economic and social transformation.

About ESCWA and Its Mission

ESCWA is the regional arm of the United Nations Secretariat serving the Arab region. It works closely with its member States to advance sustainable development, regional cooperation, and inclusive growth.

ESCWA plays a critical role in:

  • Supporting the implementation of the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development
  • Providing policy analysis and advisory services
  • Facilitating intergovernmental dialogue and cooperation
  • Delivering technical assistance to member States

Through this fellowship, ESCWA aims to cultivate a new generation of young professionals equipped with the analytical and policymaking skills needed to address complex regional challenges.

Fellowship Overview

The ESCWA Fellowship Programme 2026 provides selected fellows with practical, on-the-job experience within a United Nations environment.

Key Details

  • Programme Duration: 7 months (starting June 1, 2026)
  • Location: ESCWA Headquarters, Beirut, Lebanon (with initial remote option)
  • Monthly Stipend: USD 4,000
  • Travel Support: Round-trip economy airfare
  • Deadline: April 30, 2026 (11:59 PM Beirut time)

This structured experience enables fellows to contribute directly to ESCWA’s work while developing advanced professional competencies.

Thematic Areas of the Fellowship

ESCWA invites applications from candidates with expertise or interest in a wide range of development-related fields aligned with its strategic priorities.

Key Areas Include:

  • Macroeconomic policy
  • Trade policy and regional economic integration
  • Transport economics and infrastructure development
  • Economic modeling
  • Economic and environmental statistics
  • Social policy and inclusive development
  • Public administration and governance
  • Natural resource management and climate policy
  • Data analysis
  • Other related fields aligned with ESCWA’s work

These thematic areas reflect the multidisciplinary nature of ESCWA’s mission and provide fellows with diverse opportunities to contribute.

Roles and Responsibilities of Fellows

Selected fellows will work under the supervision of ESCWA experts and will be assigned to specific divisions or offices.

Core Responsibilities

Fellows will:

  • Develop a work programme aligned with ESCWA priorities
  • Collect, analyze, and interpret economic and social data
  • Support research and analytical studies
  • Contribute to technical reports, policy briefs, and knowledge products
  • Participate in workshops, expert meetings, and seminars
  • Assist in technical cooperation activities
  • Produce a substantive analytical output (e.g., research paper or policy brief)

This hands-on engagement ensures that fellows gain practical experience in real-world policymaking and development work.

Competencies Required

ESCWA seeks candidates who demonstrate strong professional and interpersonal competencies.

Key Competencies

  1. Professionalism
    • Strong understanding of socioeconomic and political issues
    • Ability to conduct independent research
    • Analytical and problem-solving skills
    • Commitment to deadlines and high-quality output
  2. Communication
    • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
    • Ability to present complex ideas clearly
    • Effective engagement with diverse audiences
  3. Teamwork
    • Collaboration with multicultural teams
    • Respect for diversity and inclusion
    • Ability to contribute to shared goals

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the ESCWA Fellowship Programme, applicants must meet the following requirements:

Education

  • Advanced university degree (Master’s or PhD) in:
    • Economics
    • Development studies
    • Public policy
    • Statistics
    • Governance
    • Sustainable development
    • Or related fields

Experience

  • Demonstrated research or professional experience in:
    • Economic or development policy
    • Data analysis
    • Public sector governance
    • Related ESCWA priority areas

Language Requirements

  • Fluency in English (required)
  • Knowledge of Arabic (desirable)

Special Conditions

  • Applicants must be nationals of ESCWA member States
  • Applicants must be 35 years old or younger
  • Preference is given to candidates from least developed countries in the region, including:
    • Djibouti
    • Mauritania
    • Somalia
    • Sudan
    • Yemen

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit the following documents via email:

  1. UN Administrative Profile (AP) in PDF format (including motivation statement)
  2. Research proposal (maximum 1,000 words)
  3. Copies of academic degrees
  4. Copy of passport
  5. Two letters of recommendation

Applicants are required to create their Administrative Profile through the UN Inspira system and attach it as a PDF.

Fellowship Conditions and Benefits

The ESCWA Fellowship offers comprehensive support to selected candidates.

Benefits Include:

  • Monthly stipend of USD 4,000
  • Round-trip airfare to Beirut
  • Exposure to UN systems and policymaking processes
  • Participation in regional and international meetings
  • Opportunity to publish research outputs

Additional Notes:

  • Fellows must arrange their own medical insurance
  • Residence support will be provided for long-term stays
  • Fellowship may be extended based on performance and funding availability

Logistics and Work Modality

  • Fellows are expected to relocate to Beirut upon clearance
  • Remote participation may be allowed under exceptional circumstances
  • Fellows will work within ESCWA divisions aligned with their expertise

Why You Should Apply

This fellowship provides a rare opportunity to:

  • Work within the United Nations system
  • Contribute to real-world policy and development initiatives
  • Gain international exposure and experience
  • Build a strong professional network
  • Develop advanced research and analytical skills

It is an ideal platform for young professionals aiming to influence policy and drive sustainable development in the Arab region.

Application Process

To apply:

  • Prepare all required documents
  • Submit via email to: escwa-fellowship@un.org
  • Use subject line: “ESCWA Fellowship Programme for Young Arab Professionals, 2026 Cohort”

Applicants are advised to begin early, especially for preparing the research proposal and Administrative Profile.

Click HERE to learn more and to apply now!

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply is:

April 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM (Beirut time)

Late submissions will not be considered.

Discover more of other global Fellowship opportunities on OFY.

The post ESCWA Paid Fellowship Programme 2026 for Young Arab Professionals: Fully Funded UN Opportunity with $4,000 Monthly Stipend appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Global Migration Institute Fellowships 2026: Apply for Externally Funded Postdoctoral Opportunities in Canada
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Global Migration Institute at Toronto Metropolitan University has announced a call for Expressions of Interest (EOI) for externally funded postdoctoral fellowships. This opportunity is designed to attract high-quality researchers working in migration and integration studies and support them in securing prestigious international fellowship funding. With a structured mentorship and application support system, this initiative […]

The post Global Migration Institute Fellowships 2026: Apply for Externally Funded Postdoctoral Opportunities in Canada appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Global Migration Institute at Toronto Metropolitan University has announced a call for Expressions of Interest (EOI) for externally funded postdoctoral fellowships. This opportunity is designed to attract high-quality researchers working in migration and integration studies and support them in securing prestigious international fellowship funding.

With a structured mentorship and application support system, this initiative offers a valuable pathway for early-career researchers aiming to advance their academic careers while contributing to critical global migration debates.

Overview of the Fellowship Opportunity

The Global Migration Institute (GMI) invites researchers from around the world to submit an Expression of Interest to be considered for institutional support in applying for externally funded postdoctoral fellowships.

Rather than being a direct funding program, this initiative serves as a facilitation and support mechanism. Successful applicants will receive expert guidance from GMI to strengthen their fellowship applications and improve their chances of securing funding from globally recognized programs.

The deadline to submit an Expression of Interest to GMI is April 26, 2026.

What the Institute Offers

Applicants selected through the EOI process will benefit from:

  • Institutional affiliation with a leading migration research institute
  • Tailored mentorship and feedback on fellowship applications
  • Guidance in identifying suitable external funding opportunities
  • Support in refining research proposals to meet competitive standards
  • Integration into an interdisciplinary research environment

This support significantly enhances the likelihood of success in highly competitive postdoctoral funding schemes.

Thematic Research Areas

The Global Migration Institute is seeking proposals aligned with its core research priorities. Applicants must demonstrate how their work contributes to one or more of the following thematic areas:

1. Cities and Migration

Research exploring how migration shapes urban spaces, governance, housing, and social dynamics in cities.

2. Citizenship and Belonging

Studies examining identity, inclusion, exclusion, and the socio-political dimensions of citizenship.

3. Governance of Migration in a Globalizing World

Work focused on migration policies, international cooperation, and regulatory frameworks shaping migration flows.

4. Labour Migration

Research addressing migrant labor markets, employment conditions, and economic integration.

5. Migration and Health

Projects investigating health outcomes, access to healthcare, and the broader public health implications of migration.

Applicants are encouraged to clearly articulate how their research aligns with these priority areas and contributes to advancing knowledge in migration studies.

Application Process

To apply, candidates must submit their application through the official online form by 11:59 PM EDT on April 26, 2026.

Required Application Materials

Applicants must prepare and submit the following:

  • Short Curriculum Vitae (2–4 pages)
    This should highlight academic background, publications, research experience, and relevant achievements.
  • Research Proposal Outline (1–2 pages)
    The proposal must:
    • Clearly define the research topic and objectives
    • Demonstrate alignment with one or more of GMI’s thematic priorities
    • Show the significance and potential impact of the proposed research

The selection process is highly competitive. Only a limited number of applicants will be shortlisted for the next stage.

Selection and Shortlisting

Following the review process:

  • Only a small group of applicants will be shortlisted
  • Shortlisted candidates will receive further support to develop full fellowship applications
  • Notifications are expected by mid-May 2026

Applicants are strongly advised to carefully review the eligibility requirements of their intended external fellowship programs before submitting their Expression of Interest.

Available Fellowship Programs

Selected candidates will be supported in applying to one or more of the following prestigious funding schemes:

  • SSHRC Postdoctoral Fellowships
  • Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions Global Postdoctoral Fellowships
  • NSERC Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026
  • CIHR Fellowship Program
  • Mitacs Programs
  • Toronto Metropolitan University Postdoctoral Fellowships for Black Scholars

Each of these programs has its own eligibility criteria, funding structure, and application timelines, making early preparation and institutional support especially valuable.

Why This Opportunity Matters

This call is particularly beneficial for researchers seeking:

  • Access to world-class academic mentorship
  • A strong institutional host for fellowship applications
  • Increased competitiveness in global funding programs
  • Opportunities to engage with interdisciplinary migration research

By providing structured support rather than direct funding, the Global Migration Institute helps bridge the gap between promising research ideas and successful fellowship awards.

Prospective applicants should:

  • Ensure their research aligns clearly with GMI’s thematic areas
  • Carefully prepare a concise yet compelling proposal outline
  • Verify eligibility for their chosen external fellowship programs
  • Submit all required documents before the deadline

Given the competitive nature of the process, strong alignment, clarity of research vision, and preparedness are essential for success.

This opportunity represents a strategic entry point into globally competitive postdoctoral fellowships, backed by the expertise and institutional strength of the Global Migration Institute at Toronto Metropolitan University.

Discover more similar opportunities here.

The post Global Migration Institute Fellowships 2026: Apply for Externally Funded Postdoctoral Opportunities in Canada appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: CSIS Americas Program Internship 2026: Paid Policy Internship in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Americas Program Internship (Summer 2026) at the Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS)—a leading global policy think tank based in Washington, D.C., United States. This is a competitive, paid internship designed for individuals interested in international affairs, policy research, and regional dynamics across Latin America and the Caribbean. […]

The post CSIS Americas Program Internship 2026: Paid Policy Internship in Washington, D.C. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Americas Program Internship (Summer 2026) at the Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS)—a leading global policy think tank based in Washington, D.C., United States.

This is a competitive, paid internship designed for individuals interested in international affairs, policy research, and regional dynamics across Latin America and the Caribbean.

About the Internship

The CSIS Americas Program focuses on key issues shaping the Western Hemisphere, including:

Democratic resilience
Citizen security
Economic competitiveness
Regional integration
Energy transitions
Global influence of Latin America and the Caribbean

As an intern, you will work in a fast-paced, collaborative environment supporting research, events, and policy engagement.

Internship Details

Duration: May 26 – September 10, 2026
Hours: Full-time (35 hours per week)
Location: Washington, D.C. (in-person)

Compensation

Interns are paid hourly based on education level:

$18/hour – Undergraduate students
$19/hour – Bachelor’s degree holders
$20/hour – Master’s degree holders

What You’ll Do

Key responsibilities include:

Supporting research and policy projects
Compiling literature reviews, summaries, and reports
Assisting with event planning and execution
Editing publications and drafting communications
Monitoring regional developments in the Western Hemisphere
Supporting outreach and communications activities

What They’re Looking For

Strong candidates will demonstrate:

A strong academic background in international affairs or related fields
Excellent research, analytical, and writing skills
Ability to work independently and manage multiple tasks
Interest in Latin America and the Caribbean
Fluency in Spanish (required); Portuguese is a plus
Strong communication and organizational skills

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants should have:

A bachelor’s degree (or be currently pursuing one) in a relevant field
Experience in research, writing, or office environments
Authorization to work in the United States

Why You Should Apply

This internship offers hands-on experience at one of the world’s leading policy institutions, providing exposure to real-world research, policymaking, and international affairs.

It is an excellent opportunity for anyone looking to build a career in public policy, global development, or international relations.

Application Deadline

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, but must be submitted by April 17, 2026.

How to Apply

Submit your application (CV + cover letter) via the official portal.

For more internship roles, Visit OFY here.

The post CSIS Americas Program Internship 2026: Paid Policy Internship in Washington, D.C. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
